title: tapeinia crocata
intro: Tapeinia crocata, also known as the 'New Zealand broom' or 'tai po', is a flowering plant native to New Zealand. It has been used in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to treat various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations. The plant contains bioactive compounds that provide health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making it a potential natural remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of Tapeinia crocata can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing for a range of applications in traditional medicine.
